top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Yorkshire Sportive's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
6,890ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Tour de Yorkshire Sportive's paved — English country roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Yorkshire Sportive. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,890ft of climbing on paved — English country roads does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Tour de Yorkshire Sportive, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved — English country roads at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved — English country roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour de Yorkshire Sportive’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 87 miles of paved — english country roads in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.
